title: crupina vulgaris var. brachypappa
intro: Crupina vulgaris var. brachypappa is an annual or biennial plant in the sunflower family, also known as a thistle or a weed, native to Europe and Western Asia, but has become an invasive species in other parts of the world. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are rich in sesquiterpene lactones and have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Crupina vulgaris var. brachypappa include its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Crupina vulgaris var. brachypappa include infusions of the roots, which can be made into teas or used as a topical treatment for various skin conditions.
